events.description: Intel announced major restructuring: ~15,000 job cuts, dividend suspension, capex reduction. Filed with SEC Aug 2024.
Pat Gelsinger stepped down as Intel CEO, Dec 2024. Board appointed interim co-CEOs David Zinsner and Michelle Johnston Holthaus.
Microsoft announced $10B additional investment in OpenAI, deepening Azure AI partnership.
Apple announced John Giannandrea will retire in spring 2026. Amar Subramanya named VP of AI, reporting to Craig Federighi, leading Apple Foundation Models, ML research, and AI Safety.
US Commerce Department issued updated export controls requiring licenses for NVIDIA A100, H100, and L40S chips exported to China and over 40 other countries.
AMD launched the Instinct MI300X AI accelerator, claiming superior large-language-model inference performance versus NVIDIA H100 at lower memory bandwidth cost.
US District Judge Amit Mehta ruled Google violated US antitrust laws, maintaining an illegal monopoly in the search and search text advertising markets.
Meta released Llama 3 (8B and 70B parameter models) as open-source weights, intensifying competition with closed-model AI providers including OpenAI and Anthropic.
Amazon completed a $4 billion investment in Anthropic and named AWS as Anthropic's primary cloud and training partner for Claude models.
Microsoft completed its $69 billion acquisition of Activision Blizzard following FTC litigation and UK CMA approval, becoming the third-largest gaming company by revenue.
Palantir reported its first full quarter of GAAP profitability in Q3 2023, with US commercial revenue growing 33% year-over-year, driven by its AI Platform (AIP).
Snowflake CEO Frank Slootman announced retirement; Sridhar Ramaswamy, former Google VP Advertising, appointed as incoming CEO effective immediately.
Salesforce reported Q1 FY2025 revenue of $9.13 billion with weaker-than-expected forward guidance, citing elongated enterprise sales cycles and customer budget caution around AI tooling.
Broadcom completed its $69 billion acquisition of VMware after clearing EU and Chinese regulatory approvals, immediately announcing plans to refocus VMware on private cloud.
Oracle announced deployment of NVIDIA H100 GPU supercomputer clusters on Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI), targeting AI training workloads and competing directly with AWS, Azure, and GCP.
Intel and Microsoft announced a multi-year agreement for Intel Foundry to manufacture custom chips for Microsoft on the Intel 18A process node, Intel's most advanced process.
